title: citrus planchonii
intro: Citrus planchonii, also known as the Cretan or Greek bitter orange, is a species of citrus plant native to the island of Crete. It is a type of evergreen shrub or small tree with fragrant flowers and edible fruit, and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant is valued for its antiseptic, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and skin conditions. Citrus planchonii can be prepared as herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is often used in traditional folk medicine to promote overall health and well-being.
